8AM: Here we are again. Events 8-Meridian: Strong Ely breezes. His column says Force 3. A strong breeze is Beaufort Force 6. On the few occasions that I have seen an Event wind force and a corresponding Force Column value., the value is often 1/2 of what is recorded in the Force column. When I have had to use the weather in the Event columns, I am using the Beaufort Force as indicated by the word description.

People: 1240: At 12:40 Brazilian Commodore visited this ship, and at 1:00 P.M. when firing the salute, at the 8th round, No. 2 Port gun exploded prematurely and blew off the left hand of Joseph Watt, 1st Loader and the right hand of Thomas Conroy, 1st sponger, blowing the latter overboard, who was picked up by the Brazilian Commodore's Barge, and conveyed on shore. Jos. Watt had his arm amputated on board, and was afterward conveyed on shore to the Marine Hospital.
